entry.title: What's New in SAP Subscription Billing (Q1 2026)
entry.content:
Welcome to our quarterly series highlighting the latest features in SAP Subscription Billing. This edition covers key enhancements released in the first quarter of 2026, bringing improvements in revenue analytics, data management, and pricing flexibility. Let's dive into what's new.

Recurring Revenue Analytics in Subscription Analytics Dashboard

Understanding your recurring revenue is essential for forecasting and strategic planning. The Subscription Analytics app now includes dedicated analytics for recurring revenue, giving you deeper insights into the predictable income generated from your active subscriptions. 

Two new pages are available: Recurring Revenue - Totals and Recurring Revenue - New and Churned. These pages provide comprehensive overviews and trend analysis of your recurring revenue within specific periods.

You can analyze key performance indicators including:

  • Current total recurring revenue
  • New recurring revenue
  • Revenue churn
  • Average recurring revenue per subscription

Recurring revenue analytics help you identify growth patterns, forecast future revenues based on existing recurring streams, and make informed decisions about budgeting, investment, and business scaling. 

The charts and key figures show trends and growth rates per period, and you can adjust the base period filter to view data by month, quarter, or year. The recurring revenue calculations normalize subscription amounts to daily values and then multiply by the number of days in your selected base period, ensuring accurate representation across different time frames.

Find out more at Subscription Analytics - Recurring Revenue.

 

 

 

 

 

Simplified Editing of Lookup Table Data

Managing your price conditions just got easier. The Manage Lookup Table Data app now allows you to edit individual rows directly, making quick adjustments to your price conditions without unnecessary complexity.

When you enter edit mode, you can modify any details. Need to adjust a price condition? Simply click on the price condition type to access the price condition builder, where you can fine-tune elements like price type, amount, and pricing unit.

For detailed information on managing data in lookup tables, see Lookup Tables.

 

 

 

 

Exclusion Groups for Refining Pricing Configuration

Pricing flexibility reaches a new level with exclusion groups in the Manage Pricing Schemes app. This feature allows you to compare multiple pricing steps and automatically select the most appropriate one based on specific criteria.

Four exclusion procedures are available to determine prices in different ways:

  • First Value in the Group: Uses only the first of the compared values
  • Last Value in the Group: Uses only the last of the compared values
  • Least Favorable Amount: Uses only the highest value or lowest discount among compared values
  • Most Favorable Amount: Uses only the lowest value or highest discount among compared values

Exclusion procedures with first/last values work well when comparing two pricing scheme steps. For example, you can use only two steps: one for the discounted price, and one for the normal price. By including both steps in an exclusion group and applying the first/last value procedure, you can conveniently control the pricing structure depending on the scenario. 

Procedures with least/most favorable amount are ideal for scenarios with more than two steps. For example, you can offer more favorable pricing for larger product volumes. In this case, you can create multiple price element specifications for the different volumes and set up the exclusion groups so that always the most favorable option is used in the subscription, depending on the scenario.

You can review how exclusion groups impact pricing in the Analyze Pricing Documents app.

That wraps up the summary of the most important features delivered in Q1 2026! We hope these enhancements empower your business operations. For further details, visit our What's New page on SAP Help Portal.

We encourage you to share your feedback, ask questions, or follow the SAP Subscription Billing tag to stay updated.

Until next quarter, keep innovating!
Katherine

More Information About SAP Subscription Billing

  • What’s New
  • SAP Help Portal Page
  • Feature Overview

entry.title: SAP S/4 Public How to assign a specific employee to an operation in a Maintenance order?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am currently working on a Maintenance Management implementation in S/4HANA Public Cloud and I’m looking for some guidance regarding Personnel Assignment at the Operation level and Capacity Leveling.

Current Scenario: We need to assign specific employees to Maintenance Order operations (e.g., assigning a specific task to "Sergio Bravo") and subsequently monitor their individual capacity/availability to execute the work.

What we have done so far:

  • We have configured and assigned employees using the app "Manage Teams and Responsibilities - For Work Center Asset Management" for a specific Work Center.

  • However, we are unable to see these assignments reflected in the standard maintenance apps or find a way to filter/view individual capacity based on these assignments.

Technical Questions:

  1. Is it possible to perform individual person-level assignment and capacity monitoring without Scope Item 43R (Resource Scheduling)?

  2. If Scope Item 43R is the only way to achieve this through the Maintenance Scheduling Board or Manage Work Center Capacity apps, are there any workarounds within the standard Core for tracking specific personnel availability?

  3. In the absence of 43R, what is the best practice for visibility of assigned technicians to specific operations in Public Cloud?

Any insights, documentation, or "Best Practices" regarding this specific requirement would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Sergio Bravo

entry.title: Extending Standard S/4 HANA OData API's
entry.content:
Introduction

Recently we build a solution to publish product data to a Kafka Event Broker, allowing consumer system to subscribe to different materials events based on the product type.  As part of the solution we wanted to make sure we keep a clean core principle, enable new standard enterprise events and API in the S/4 HANA backend, and evaluate the best alternatives to enrich and distribute the events considering the landscape available to the customer: S/4 2023 FP1, Integration Suite, Event Mesh, Advanced Event Mesh, and Kafka.

After delivering the initials solution, there was a clear gap for the business: "Where are our material master custom fields? "

I am not an ABAP developer, but I wanted to find out if the promise of easy extensibility is delivered. 
Spoiler: It will take you longer to read this blog, than execute the extension.

This blog show simplest approach to extend a standard OData the API. If you are curious about whole solution, comment on the blog and encourage me to blog more details on the other components too.

I will be using Product API for the example and screenshots, but the process should be applicable to most API too.

1. Enterprise Events and API

Default Enterprise Event in S/4 HANA are by default notification events. Their role is to notify of a change of state of an object, but the message will only include key parameter and not the context data for the object.
This also meant that for every standard event there is at-least one an associated API to extract the context data of the object.

Starting at the events is important, because this perspective will help you identify which API would the best for you to work with.

Familiarize yourself with Business Accelerator Hub: 
This link is for S/4 HANA On-premise: https://api.sap.com/products/SAPS4HANA/events/events

Here you can see the operations and schemas for events, if you are curious

 

Navigate to Documents, this will take you to the sap help documentation and is how you find the API related to the events

Make sure you select the correct version and feature pack as you landscape has.

 

2. OData V2 vs OData V4

Prefer ODATA V4 API when available. 

In the example above the related API is "Product Master (A2X)" is a Odata V2 implementation, but the system also has a OData V4 implementation.  OData V4 is always preferred as they are:

  • OData V4 implementation are more compliant to the OData OASIS specification
  • The service enablement process is simpler and will not require a custom interface object the the customer namespace. 
  • Has more extensibility option that OData V2 predecessors.

 for example: 

API

Product Master (A2X)

Product (Version 2)

Service Enablement TCODESERVICE_ENABLEMENT

SERVICE_ENABLEMENT

Maintenance TCODE

/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE - Activate and Maintain Services

 

/IWFND/V4_ADMIN - SAP Gateway Service Administration 

 

3. Extensibility Capability of APIs

In the SAP Help Documentation Find the Extensibility section for the API, and note of the object where you want to extend the API, in our case the custom field will be part of the main Product Entity.

This is important because you will need those objects when you  design the ABAP extensions

Technical name

Product Master (A2X)

Product (Version 2)

Extensibility 

5 Extensibility objects

 Note: A_Product

24 Extensibility objects

Note: A_Product_3

4. Eclipse and ABAP Developer Tools

The extensibility of standard API is by default possible using Eclipse and ABAP development tools. Maybe some ABAP gurus still can do a clean core extension in the ABAP workbench but I recommend to follow SAP recommendation to use ADT.

To download Eclipse and install the ABAP development tools follow this link: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#abap

Assuming you have developer roles in the sandbox or developments system, and a developer key.

You should be able to add you ABAP system and to extend an API. 

 

You will need to create a new Data Definition  as under Core data Services. 
 

Use template  extendView, or extendViewEntity, if you are not sure start with extendViewEntity, you can always change the code template later or create a new one. 

5. Drilling down to the right extension point

In our example we want to extend the entity 

  • A_Product_3

With Field that already exist in table MARA.

extend view entity A_Product_3 with {
  mara.zmcat as ExtendedMaterialCategory,
  mara.zmcat_stat as ExtendedMaterialCategoryStatus
}

But you will see an error here, the table MARA cannot be found in entity A_Product_3, you will need to drill down and extend the additional entities and views on each layer.  

To start put your mouse over A_Product_3, open the context menu and select Navigate to  or Press F3

 

In the code for you will notices 3 things: 

  • The view entity is a  projection of R_ProductTP
  • Defined as with the alias "Product"
  • Still does not have MARA defined

 

Navigate to R_ProductTP

  • Is a root view selecting from interface view I_Product
  • still MARA is not defined, therefore no access to the custom fields.

Navigate to I_Product

  • I_Product is an interface this is the view selecting from mara
  • Jackpot 😀

6. Deploy and test.

In conclusion We will need to add 3 core data extensions:

  1. extendView : I_Product
  2. extendViewEntity: R_ProductTP
  3. extendViewEntity: A_Product_3
//ZI_PRODUCT_EXT

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZI_PRODUCT_EXT'
@EndUserText.label: 'Base Extension for I_Product'
extend view I_Product with ZI_PRODUCT_EXT
{
    mara.zmcat,
    mara.zmcat_stat
}
//ZR_PRODUCTTP_EXT

extend view entity R_ProductTP with {
  I_Product.zmcat,
  I_Product.zmcat_stat
}
//ZA_PRODUCT_3_EXT

extend view entity A_Product_3 with {
  Product.zmcat as ExtendedMaterialCategory,
  Product.zmcat_stat as ExtendedMaterialCategoryStatus
}

After successful deployment - test the API and you will see the extended field part of it.😎

Follow the same approach for extending other API with customer fields.
And the approach is similar if you need to extend with logic, associations or compositions.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2511 - MAIF Migration Program
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently in the process of migrating from SSAM 2405 to SSAM 2511 and are using the report /SMFND/CORE_MIGRATE_MAPP_CONFG to migrate the mobile application configuration.

We would appreciate your guidance on the following input parameters required by the program:

Source Application Pattern
Should this be the exact name of the existing (source) mobile application from SSAM 2405?
Or does it support wildcards/pattern-based selection (e.g., prefix-based)?
Target Application Pattern
Should this be the name of the new application in SSAM 2511?
Is it expected to already exist, or will the program create it during migration?
Source App Feature/Persona Prefix
What value should be provided here?
Is this meant to match existing feature/persona IDs (e.g., prefixes used in configuration tables), or is it used for transformation during migration?
Custom Objects Handling
Will the migration program also consider custom OMDOs and OData models created in the source application?
Are there any limitations or additional steps required to ensure custom configurations are migrated correctly?
Additionally, if there are any best practices or prerequisites (e.g., naming conventions, transport considerations, or known constraints) for running this migration program, please let us know.

I have reviewed the SAP documentation; however, some aspects are still unclear.

https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/upgrading-sap-service-and-asset-manager/migrating-custom-configurations-to-newer-version-of-mobile-application?locale=en-US

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: User Assistance Is Here To Help You!
entry.content:
Hi Kerisha,

To my knowledge, there's no such feature planned for Fiori apps (aka UI5 apps). 

However, if data elements are assigned to UI elements in Fiori apps and long texts are available, the long texts are displayed in the Help Panel. This feature has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 and SAP S/4HANA /SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025. This doesn't mean that the Performance Assistant is no longer available. You can always call it up by positioning the cursor on a specific field and using the <FN>+<F1> keyboard combination. In the Performance Assistant, you can then call up the technical details of the field.

Hope this helps.

Best regards,

Francesco

entry.title: Requirement of Custom Workflow of Integration of SD & PP Module In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We received one requirement which need to map on SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud. The requirement is complex and in case of MTO which the SD & PP integration.

Please help and suggest the solution.

Requirement 

Required custom work flow to map MTO process with approval of 3 level the requirement is custom as no specific standard workflow available in SAP Public cloud after analysis sharing this with you 

Below are the discussed way-forward

1. Export End-to-End Process Flow

Step 1: Sales Order Creation

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales Order App
  • Mandatory Fields:
    • Export Order Type
    • Order Date
    • Customer Delivery Date (Mandatory)
  • Enhancement:
    • Add validation prevent save without delivery date

Step 2: Auto Notification to Stakeholders

Once order is saved:

Trigger Workflow / Notification to: Configuration basis.

  • Export Head
  • Procurement Manager
  • Logistics Manager
  • Production Manager
  • Export Billing Team
  • MD / COO

2. Department-wise Action Flow

Procurement (RM/PM Availability)

  • SAP Inbox Task
  • Action:
    • YES → RM/PM Available
    • NO → Enter Expected Availability Date

Production Planning

  • Triggered after Procurement update

Production Manager updates:

  • Production Start Date
  • Production Completion Date
  • FG Deposit Date

Logistics & Billing

After production completion:

  • Logistics:
    • Shipment Planning Date
    • Dispatch Date
  • Billing:
    • Invoice Date
    • Export Documentation Status

3. Status Tracking Structure

Create Status Flow Table



Stage

Status

Order Created

Completed

RM/PM Availability

Pending / Confirmed

Production

Planned / In Progress / Completed

Logistics

Planned / Dispatched

Billing

Completed

Final Status

Closed

4. Delay Alert Mechanism

Requirement:

If any department delays → Alert to ALL stakeholders

  • SAP Alert Notification Service

Logic:

  • Define SLA for each stage:
    • Procurement → 1 day
    • Production Planning → 2 days
    • Logistics → 1 day

If delay:

  • Trigger:
    • Email Alert
    • Fiori Notification
    • Escalation to MD / COO

Reporting:

  • Dashboard:
    • Order-wise status
    • Delay tracking
    • Department-wise performance

7. Workflow Logic (Simplified)

Order Created
   ↓
Notify All Stakeholders
   ↓
Procurement Update
   ↓
Production Planning Update
   ↓
Production Completion
   ↓
Logistics Planning
   ↓
Billing
   ↓
Order Closed

8. Automation Enhancements (Recommended)

  • Auto reminders every 6 / 12 / 24 hrs
  • Escalation Matrix:
    • Level 1 → Department Head
    • Level 2 → COO
    • Level 3 → MD

Final Outcome Expectation:

We will get:

  • Complete visibility of Export Orders
  • Department accountability
  • Automatic escalation system
  • Real-time tracking dashboard

Looking forward to hearing solution from you.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Looking for Standard SAP Solution for Master Data Creation Requests with Approval Workflow
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to ask for guidance on whether SAP provides a standard solution for handling master data creation/change requests. Specifically for Equipment, including an approval workflow and automated creation in the system.

Current Situation in Our Company

We have a Master Data Coordinator who is responsible for creating and updating master data in SAP. Our current process looks like this:

  1. A user wants to create or change an equipment.
  2. The user fills out a manual form/template explaining the required data and the reason for the request.
  3. The user submits this form into an approval workflow in SAP PLM.
  4. After all approvals are completed, the Master Data Coordinator manually:
    • Opens the form
    • Copies/pastes all values into SAP
    • Creates or updates the equipment

Challenges

  • Manual copy/paste introduces a high risk of human error.
  • The process is slow and depends heavily on one person.
  • We want to reduce manual work and improve data quality.

What We Want to Achieve

We are looking for a standard SAP process or Fiori app that would allow:

  1. A user submits a request to create an equipment directly in SAP (via Fiori or another standard interface).
  2. The request to go through an approval workflow.
  3. After approval, the system should automatically create the equipment in SAP without manual intervention.

Essentially, we want to replace the manual form + manual data entry with a more integrated, automated, and error‑free process.

My Questions to the Community

  • Does SAP offer a standard Fiori app or standard workflow for equipment creation/change requests?
  • Is there a standard SAP component (e.g., PLM, EAM-specific apps) that supports:
    • Request submission
    • Approval workflow
    • Automatic creation of equipment after approval
  • If yes, what would be the recommended approach?

Why This Matters

We want to avoid building a large custom solution if SAP already provides something standard. Our goal is to:

  • Improve data quality
  • Reduce manual work
  • Minimize errors
  • Streamline the approval process

Any insights, recommendations, or experiences from other companies would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud and Azure integration for SSL (Let’s Encrypt)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working on a SAP Commerce Cloud project where the storefront is currently exposed directly via a custom domain.

We are evaluating how to manage SSL certificates , ideally using Let’s Encrypt for automated renewal.

At the moment, it seems that Commerce Cloud does not provide native support for automated certificate management, and manual upload is required.

In parallel, we also have an application running on Microsoft Azure (App Service).

My question is:

Is there a recommended way to leverage Azure in front of SAP Commerce Cloud to handle SSL termination and certificate management?

Any guidance or experience would be appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: AGP’s Next Chapter: Dual SAP Certification Fuels Cloud Growth Ambitions
entry.content:
AGP, a specialized SAP service provider based in Chile, has reached an important milestone in its growth journey: achieving its first-ever certifications for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Operations and SAP BTP Operations. But for AGP, this achievement goes beyond formal recognition. It reflects a deliberate effort to strengthen quality, standardize processes, and further mature as an organization. In this interview, AGP shares what motivated them to pursue certification at this stage, how the audit process catalyzed internal improvement, and why they see certification as a foundation for long-term excellence rather than a one-time validation. The conversation also explores AGP’s expanding role in cloud transformation - building capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation - and outlines its vision to become a leading SAP partner in the region.

Could you start by telling us about your company – what services do you offer, and which industries or customer types do you primarily serve?

AGP is a specialized SAP service provider focused on implementing, supporting, and optimizing solutions such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP. We primarily work with large-scale organizations across industries such as healthcare, mining, transportation, and services, where operational quality and business continuity are critical. Our main differentiator is our team: highly committed professionals with international experience who work closely with our clients, understand their businesses, and act as strategic partners.

AGP team

 

You were recently certified for the first time for SAP SuccessFactors HCM operations and SAP BTP operations. What motivated you to pursue these certifications at this point in your journey?

The certification represents a natural step in our evolution. We have been strengthening our SAP operations with a strong focus on quality, processes, and specialization, and these certifications formally validate that path. Our main motivation was to reassure our clients that we operate under SAP-recognized standards, reinforcing our value proposition based on trust, excellence, and delivery capability.

 

Looking back, how did you experience the audit process? And did it give you any new perspectives – whether on your internal governance, quality standards, or ways of working?

The process was very enriching. It allowed us to confirm that our practices are aligned with international standards, while also identifying opportunities for improvement in governance, documentation, and standardization. More than an audit, we experienced it as a step forward in organizational maturity, reinforcing our commitment to continuous improvement.

 

SAP is strongly focused on the cloud. How are you already supporting customers in their migration, and where do you plan to expand?

We are already supporting our customers in their transition to the cloud, especially SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP solutions. We participate in both implementation and AMS services, ensuring the solutions' continuity, evolution, and adoption. Our expansion focus is on strengthening capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation on SAP BTP, as well as enabling more agile and scalable cloud operating models.

How do you plan to differentiate yourself and leverage the certification – both in the market and in your communication with customers?

Our differentiation goes beyond the certification itself - it lies in how we work. The certification supports a model based on close client relationships, high-level teams, and a strong service culture. We will leverage it to reinforce market trust, demonstrating that we combine SAP standards with a human, flexible, and results-oriented approach.

Where do you see the biggest growth opportunities, and how do you plan to scale your team to meet demand?

We see significant opportunities in cloud adoption, especially in talent management solutions and innovation platforms like SAP BTP. To scale, we are strongly investing in local talent development, training and certifying new consultants, while strengthening our team with senior profiles who bring experience and culture. We believe sustainable growth is achieved by building strong teams, not just increasing capacity.

What are your strategic plans to deepen your partnership with SAP in the coming years?

Our goal is to position ourselves as a leading partner in the region. We aim to continue expanding our certifications, strengthening our cloud capabilities, and co-innovating with SAP to deliver real value to our customers. Our relationship with SAP is strategic, and we see it as a long-term partnership built on trust, quality, and shared growth.

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.
